title: Carbon Black Watchlist Hit Trending
spl:
index=endpoint sourcetype="carbonblack:watchlist"
| timechart span=1h count by watchlist_id
| where count>0
description: Watchlist trending validates threat intel relevance to your estate.
value: Watchlist trending validates threat intel relevance to your estate.
implementation: Track IOC watchlist hits from intel feeds. Expire stale lists to reduce volume.
knownFalsePositives: Carbon Black alerts from new endpoint agents, patch cycles, or legitimate admin, developer, and IT automation tools; use asset groups and allowlists to narrow noise.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: `Splunk_TA_vmware_carbonblack`.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `sourcetype=carbonblack:watchlist`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Track IOC watchlist hits from intel feeds. Expire stale lists to reduce volume.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=endpoint sourcetype="carbonblack:watchlist"
| timechart span=1h count by watchlist_id
| where count>0
```

Understanding this SPL

**Carbon Black Watchlist Hit Trending** — Watchlist trending validates threat intel relevance to your estate.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype=carbonblack:watchlist`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `Splunk_TA_vmware_carbonblack`. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: endpoint; **sourcetype**: carbonblack:watchlist. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Detection type** for this use case: Hunting — interpret thresholds and fields in that context.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=endpoint, sourcetype="carbonblack:watchlist". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `timechart` plots the metric over time using **span=1h** buckets with a separate series **by watchlist_id** — ideal for trending and alerting on this use case.
• Filters the current rows with `where count>0` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.


Step 3 — Validate
Run the search over a window that includes both normal production traffic and, if you have one, a documented test (vendor sandbox, lab rule hit, or exercise). Compare a few sample events to the vendor’s own console or reports and tune indexes, fields, and thresholds so alerts are actionable before paging.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Line chart (hits), Table (watchlist, IOC).
grandmaExplanation: We use Carbon Black data and alerts to stay ahead of the risks described in "Carbon Black Watchlist Hit Trending". We help the team notice trouble early, while a situation is still small and containable.
dataModelAcceleration: After vendor logs are CIM-tagged, enable Data Model Acceleration for the CIM models listed on this use case; otherwise the optional tstats path may return no rows.
